title: Office of Space Commerce to move ahead on mission authorization
summary: The Commerce Department is advancing plans for a voluntary mission authorization system to regulate novel space activities not covered by other agencies. This initiative aims to fill regulatory gaps in emerging space missions.

title: Lightning strikes Long March 3B after liftoff, Kinetica-1 launches 5 satellites
summary: A Long March 3B rocket successfully delivered its payload to orbit despite being struck by lightning shortly after liftoff. Separately, the Kinetica-1 rocket launched five satellites.
company: China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation (CASC)

title: EU delays release of Copernicus imagery over Gulf of Oman
summary: The European Union delayed the release of certain Copernicus Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 imagery for 24 hours due to a U.S. request, citing concerns over the Gulf of Oman region.

title: Air Force takes over troubled military GPS receiver program
summary: The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center will now oversee the development, integration, and sustainment of military GPS receiver equipment. This move aims to address issues with accessing the military’s encrypted GPS signal.
company: Air Force Life Cycle Management Center

title: NASA programs feel effects of workforce reductions
summary: NASA reports that over two dozen major projects have been impacted by a 20% reduction in its civil servant workforce over the past year. The workforce cuts are affecting critical programs across the agency.

title: Poland commits $745 million to EU’s IRIS² constellation project
summary: Poland has committed $745 million to the EU's IRIS² multi-orbit satellite internet constellation project. This investment will support the development of the constellation, which aims to provide secure and resilient satellite communications.

title: Mark Matossian co-founds Whipsmart Ventures to fund space and deep tech
summary: Mark Matossian, a space startup veteran, has co-founded Whipsmart Ventures, a venture capital fund focused on space and deep tech. The fund aims to support innovative companies in the space industry.

title: Will Bruey on rethinking the role of pharmaceuticals in low Earth orbit
summary: Varda CEO Will Bruey discusses the company's role in bridging pharmaceutical development with low Earth orbit at the Ascend conference. The conversation highlights Varda's efforts to leverage space for medical advancements.

title: Airbus space business takes advantage of growing demand for satellite systems
summary: Airbus's space business has rebounded, capitalizing on increased demand for satellite systems, particularly from Europe. The company is pursuing a joint venture to further leverage this growth.

title: China’s Shijian-31 satellite is sweeping GEO using a unique orbit
summary: China's Shijian-31 satellite, launched in June, is operating in a unique orbit that provides a distinctive view of the geostationary belt. This classified satellite's creative orbital path sets it apart from typical geostationary satellites.

title: The easiest way to learn the summer night sky starts with these 3 stars
summary: A guide on learning the summer night sky using the Summer Triangle stars—Vega, Deneb, and Altair—is provided. These stars serve as a key to understanding celestial navigation.

title: Epic photo captures lightning striking a Chinese rocket during liftoff
summary: A Chinese Long March-3B rocket was struck by lightning shortly after liftoff but still successfully deployed its satellite payload into orbit. The incident was captured in a dramatic photograph.

title: This Week In Space LIVE: The Viking Mars landings 50 years later
summary: A live event titled 'This Week In Space' is being held to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the Viking Mars landings. The session will air on July 24 at 1:30 p.m. ET.

title: Hubble telescope watches unique nova explosion fire cosmic 'bullets' through the Milky Way at 20 million mph
summary: Astronomers using the Hubble telescope observed a unique helium nova explosion in the Milky Way, ejecting cosmic 'bullets' at 20 million mph. This is the only known helium nova in our galaxy.

title: Vulcan Centaur swapped out again: NASA goes with SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket for launch of space weather mission
summary: NASA's SunRISE mission will now launch on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy instead of a ULA Vulcan Centaur. The change in launch vehicle reflects ongoing adjustments in mission planning.

title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4: Release date & how to watch Paramount+’s sensational sci-fi series
summary: Paramount+ has announced the release date for 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' Season 4. The sci-fi series is set to continue its sensational storytelling with a new season.

title: Scientists may have finally solved the mystery of the sun's missing silver
summary: Scientists have revised their understanding of the sun's composition, finding a slightly higher presence of silver than previously believed. This discovery helps resolve a long-standing mystery about the sun's elemental makeup.

title: How much better is the Canon EOS R6 III than previous models for astrophotography?
summary: The Canon EOS R6 III offers 8 MP more resolution, a CFexpress card slot, and improved low-light autofocusing compared to the EOS R6 II. These upgrades may benefit astrophotographers by enhancing image detail and performance in low-light conditions.

title: NASA's retired astronauts are hitting the road to get young people to vote: 'one small step for you, and one giant leap for our country.'
summary: NASA's retired astronauts are encouraging young people to vote, framing civic engagement as a critical mission for the country's future. They plan to visit colleges and universities to spread their message.

title: Satellite spots a 'hummingbird' in Antarctica | Space photo of the day for July 23, 2026
summary: A satellite image from NISAR captured a hummingbird-like shape in Antarctica, which was later identified as a mountaintop. The photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for July 23, 2026.

title: Hubble Spies One-Sided Spiral
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the one-sided spiral galaxy NGC 4654, located 72 million light-years away in the constellation Virgo. This observation highlights the galaxy's asymmetric structure.

title: A Million-Panel Project
summary: Utah's new solar power and battery storage facility is one of the largest in the region, featuring a million-panel project. The initiative aims to enhance renewable energy capacity and grid stability.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
summary: The Curiosity rover team celebrates the contributions of engineers past and present in a recent blog post. The post highlights the work of the APXS uplink lead and strategic planner, emphasizing the instrument's role in measuring rock chemistry.

title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
summary: NASA's streaming service, NASA+, is now available on Amazon Fire TV Channels, allowing users to access content via Alexa. Future programming may include science mission launches and test flights.

title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
summary: NASA crews transported the 130-foot-tall Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank from a production cell to a test building at the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on May 15, 2026. The tank will be part of the core stage for the Artemis IV mission.

title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars
summary: NASA's MAVEN mission has revealed that auroras on Mars form similarly to those on Earth, using the same mechanism that directs charged particles into the atmosphere. The findings, published in Nature Communications, provide new insights into Martian atmospheric phenomena.

title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Closes Out Space Station Mission
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams completed an eight-month mission aboard the International Space Station, contributing to cancer research, materials science, and completing two spacewalks.

title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at Langley Research Center in Virginia on July 31. This is the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years.

title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
summary: A robotic servicing mission launched on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral, carrying the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) with the NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload to service spacecraft in geosynchronous Earth orbit.

title: Starship Flight 13 moved to Friday due to weather
summary: Starship Flight 13's launch has been postponed to Friday due to weather conditions. SpaceX will attempt the launch again on the new date.

title: Soyuz MS-29 crew arrives at ISS, U.S. EVA completes Canadarm2 repairs
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 crew successfully arrived at the International Space Station (ISS), increasing the station's crew size. A U.S. extravehicular activity (EVA) completed repairs on the Canadarm2 robotic arm.

title: Path to Starship Flight 13 requires additional Booster 20 test
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Starship Flight 13, targeting a launch no earlier than July 23, but requires additional testing of Booster 20. The delay highlights ongoing technical challenges in the Starship program.

title: Falcon 9 to launch MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is scheduled to launch the MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman. The mission aims to demonstrate in-space servicing capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is planning a new attempt for the Starship Flight 13 launch after a previous abort. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are scheduled for upcoming launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Three companies—Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space—are making progress on their new medium-to-heavy-lift rockets: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5, respectively. While SpaceX's Starship has dominated recent headlines, these developments highlight growing competition in the commercial launch sector.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for its first land recovery.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission to sustain operations and research aboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for another test flight. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: The silicon brains steering next-generation antennas
summary: The article discusses advancements in silicon-based technologies for next-generation antennas. It highlights how these innovations are driving improvements in antenna performance and efficiency.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: The European Space Agency's PLATO spacecraft has completed critical electromagnetic compatibility tests in ESTEC's Maxwell Test Chamber, confirming its electronics are ready for space. PLATO is now cleared for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket, scheduled for March 2027.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and industry stakeholders.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications. It highlights the potential of this technology to revolutionize robotic operations in space.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: The ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science activities by involving skilled research institutes.

title: ESA Puts €65M Behind Europe’s First Lunar Ice Rover
summary: ESA has committed €65 million to Europe's first lunar polar ice exploration rover mission, led by ispace-Europe. This marks a significant step for Europe's involvement in lunar exploration.

title: Lunar Outpost Plans to Fly NVIDIA Chips to the Moon
summary: Lunar Outpost is planning to send NVIDIA chips to the Moon, merging the semiconductor and lunar exploration industries. This move highlights the growing intersection of technology and space missions.

title: FCC Filings Show Eutelsat is Working on a New 528-sat Constellation Beyond OneWeb
summary: Eutelsat is developing a new constellation of 528 satellites, separate from its OneWeb program, according to FCC filings. The company's Next program appears to still be in development.

title: NASA Puts $2B Price Tag on Nuclear Mars Mission
summary: NASA has announced a $2 billion price tag for the Space Reactor-1 Freedom, a fission-powered spacecraft designed for interplanetary missions to Mars. This marks the first such mission to utilize nuclear propulsion technology.

title: Intel Authorization Act Proposes Limiting Commercial EO Access
summary: The proposed Intel Authorization Act seeks to restrict commercial Earth observation (EO) satellite operators from sharing data with the public, framing EO data as a privilege rather than a right. This move aims to increase government control over commercial EO access.

title: The UK Scraps the Department in Charge of UKSA
summary: The UK has decided to abolish the department overseeing the UK Space Agency (UKSA) during the Farnborough Air Show. This move comes as the country highlights its aerospace accomplishments.

title: The UK and Space Florida Launch Joint Fund
summary: The UK and Space Florida have launched a joint fund to support projects with partners from both regions. This initiative aims to foster collaboration between the UK and Florida in space-related ventures.

title: Breaking Down the $686B Space Economy
summary: The global space economy reached a record $686 billion in 2025, as reported by the Space Foundation. This marks significant growth in the industry's economic impact.

title: FCC Eyes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Bands for Satellites
summary: The FCC is considering a proposal to permit unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites. This move aims to expand satellite connectivity options for consumer devices.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will be used to expand manufacturing capacity and research new capabilities.

title: Space Is Coming for Silicon
summary: SpaceX has introduced Starmind, an AI satellite megaconstellation, marking a significant step in orbital data centers. Elon Musk highlighted the project as a major advancement in space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Iceye and KT SAT Partner on Flood Intelligence in South Korea
summary: Iceye and KT SAT have partnered to provide flood monitoring solutions in South Korea for government and commercial use. This is the first deployment of Iceye Solutions in the country.

title: Intellian Releases New Terminal for Iridium GMDSS Service
summary: Intellian has launched a new terminal for Iridium’s GMDSS maritime safety service, leveraging the Iridium Certus 100 service. The terminal was announced on Thursday and is designed for use with the Global Maritime Distress and Safety System (GMDSS).

title: Frequency Electronics Reports Increased Demand From Proliferated Satellite Programs
summary: Frequency Electronics reports $18 million in new contract wins, including an $11 million award from an undisclosed major aerospace prime contractor for a proliferated satellite program.

title: FCC Approves a New C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC approved a new C-band auction to clear 160 megahertz of the upper C-band spectrum from satellite operators. This auction aims to build on previous C-band spectrum reallocations.

title: Lite Coms Wins $22M Contract to Support Operational Readiness
summary: Lite Coms secured a $22 million contract from an unnamed government and defense customer to provide satellite communications for operational readiness. The company specializes in integration and manufacturing of satellite communication solutions.

title: FCC Overhauls Satellite Licensing, Adopts ‘Assembly Line’ Approach
summary: The FCC approved a major overhaul of its satellite licensing process, introducing a streamlined 'licensing assembly line' approach. This change aims to simplify and accelerate the approval of space and earth station licenses.

title: Iridium Reports 4% Revenue Growth in Q2 Amid Rocket Lab Acquisition Deal
summary: Iridium reported a 4% revenue growth in Q2 while in the midst of an acquisition deal with Rocket Lab. The satellite operator's financial performance and acquisition status were highlighted.

title: Contrivian Completes Merger With Big Network to Fuel Orchestration Service
summary: Contrivian has finalized its merger with Big Network, a networking software company, to create a vertically integrated, multi-modal connectivity platform. The deal aims to enhance orchestration services in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Launches Northrop Grumman’s First Mission Robotic Vehicle
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-MEP) on a mission to provide on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension services. The launch marks a significant step in advancing satellite servicing technologies.
